page: Microsoft Azure G4 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the G family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 224.0 GB of memory, yielding a high resource density of 14.0 GB of RAM per core. Additionally, it includes 3146 GB of local storage, with no integrated GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks reveal distinct tradeoffs between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core processing is limited, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% and bottom 25% respectively. However, multi-core performance is more robust, achieving average ratings in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory performance also shows variance; while small-block cached read bandwidth is average, large-block uncached write bandwidth is poor, placing in the bottom 10%.

Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage capacity, this server is configured for memory-intensive and data-heavy tasks. It demonstrates average performance in database operations, static web serving, and Redis SET operations. In terms of cost efficiency, the hardware profile offers a high density of RAM and local storage relative to its compute capacity, though the older CPU architecture limits its single-threaded efficiency. It is suitable for relational databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure G4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server within the G family, utilizing a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs. It features 224.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 14.0 GB per core, alongside 3146 GB of local storage. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the server exhibits a clear performance tradeoff: single-core benchmarks are weak, but multi-core and multi-threaded operations, such as Geekbench multi-core and bzip3 decompression, achieve average tier results. Memory bandwidth is average for cached small blocks but poor for uncached large-block writes. This resource profile, combining high memory capacity with significant local storage, makes the server suitable for database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive multi-threaded workloads where single-core speed is not the primary constraint.
og_description: Microsoft Azure G4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 16 vCPUs, 224 GB RAM, and 3146 GB local storage, suitable for database operations and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure G4 is an x86_64 server with 16 vCPUs, 224 GB RAM, and 3146 GB local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 server combining high memory capacity and substantial local storage for memory-intensive, multi-threaded database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores
224.0 GB of RAM providing 14.0 GB of memory per core
3146 GB of local storage for data-heavy applications
Average multi-core CPU and database operation benchmark performance
Weak single-core processing and uncached memory write speeds
Suited for relational databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded workloads
